reviews.author: Maxim CASSOVIANUS
reviews.text: The beach is nice and long, nice sand / but it's far from white /, there are palm trees but there is a lot of garbage ❗️❗️❗️ Shame, it could be paradise but the Vietnamese don't give a damn. It's just that attention to detail and making things perfect are not their priority, too bad.

reviews.author: The MY World
reviews.text: It's Sao Beach that everyone know. There were not so many people because alsmost all people move to Kem Beach, so now it's good local beach. Still it's good place to come. This is better place if you want to relax. If you want to feel "Resort", then go to Kem beach.

reviews.author: Concon mcmc
reviews.text: Thought the beach was decent. Some dirty parts but still a very usable beach. Much quieter than Khem beach and felt more rustic. Would recommend.

reviews.author: Tony Bennett
reviews.text: Could be lovely if the extortionate Sao Beach bar paid to have the beach cleaned up instead of charging tourists 60k vnd just to access the beach through their entrance and then charge 150k vnd for use of the loungers, swings and hammocks! Lots of other access points along the beach. So much rubbish along this beach though, such a shame.

reviews.author: Robert Skovran
reviews.text: We were quite disappointed. It’s true that the sea has a beautiful color + sand is clearly white, but there is a lot of trash and litter around the beach. Some sections are cleaner and you can see that they are trying to maintain them, but it still affects the overall impression. What made our visit better was Highlands Coffee – it looks great and we enjoyed a coffee with a nice sea view.

reviews.author: Zygzac
reviews.text: I would say that this beach is way better then Khem Beach, just because its wider and there are less bars, but of course it depends on the preferences. You must note that on all the beaches on islands there are plenty garbage and rubbish everywhere (in the water or on the sand). The view is nice and you can do some water sports here.

reviews.author: Victorya
reviews.text: I loved it!! White sand, waves and turquoise water😍 it wasn't as crowded as I was expecting. There are many different places with sun beds (cost 150k) and you can also just put a towel on the sand if you don't want to pay. There are many photo opportunity places and also things like jet-skies, paragliding and banana boats.

reviews.author: Anna Graf
reviews.text: A beautiful beach with turquoise water and white sand. Unfortunately, the charm is spoiled by the roaring jet ski engines, which make constant noise. I'd like to hear the roar of the water, not the engines, and the stench of gasoline. Jet skiing should be banned in such a beautiful place, and there should be a quiet zone. It really spoiled my beach experience.

reviews.author: Anja Ognjanovic
reviews.text: The beach is beautiful and well maintained. There is an entrance fee and sunbeds are paid separately, but everything is quite affordable and definitely worth it. The crowd is small, so it feels peaceful and relaxing, and the overall atmosphere is wonderful.

reviews.author: Roger
reviews.text: The beach itself is very beautiful, with nice sand and clear water. It’s especially pleasant in the morning, before 10am, it’s still relatively quiet and uncrowded. After that, it fills up quickly, and water activities start, bringing quite a bit of noise. Taking photos with hammocks or decorative setups costs around 30,000 VND, and the sun loungers look somewhat worn at first glance. We didn’t stay long, just stopped for a quick drink and moved on once busloads of visitors started arriving...

reviews.author: Gabriela Zeveleanu
reviews.text: Sao Beach A beautiful place, completely wrong managed and valorized. The beauty of the sea and of the beach is for sure destroyed by: 1. The garbage on the beach and in the sea. Full of plastic garbage everywhere! No trash bins, 0 respect for nature all around. Nobody looking or trying to collect it. 2. Noise of jet skies and boats. They are exactly in the middle of the tourists. No safety distance, no rules. 3. Smell of gasoline that makes the air toxic and impossible to breathe 4. Sun-beds crowded one next to the other. 0 intimacy, 0 personal space in the area which has sun beds. Fully understanding the need of better revenues, but on the long run, that’s for sure the receipt for bankruptcy. Fixing these 4 points, could make the real difference for Sao Beach and Vietnamese tourism.

reviews.author: Karolina Kocianova
reviews.text: I dont know if I was extremaly unlucky, but the beach and the sea is nothing like the pictures and I strongly dissagree with the statement that the beach is one of the best on the island… i have never seen such a green water, such a mess and smell🐸

reviews.author: Eleonora Spina
reviews.text: Loved this beach! There is a bit of plastic unfortunately but there are no big resorts and loud tourists. You can bring your own towel and choose your spot. I got sand flies bites (nothing dangerous) but it was my fault because I sat on the sand without towel! So be careful for that :)

reviews.author: Juraj Jankovič
reviews.text: The beach has potential, but there is an enormous amount of ocean trash everywhere and nobody cleans it. Based on the layers of trash, it’s a long-term problem that nobody is addressing. We decided to leave and go to Khem Beach instead — there are more people, but it’s much cleaner, with no trash at all.

reviews.author: Aaron Lim
reviews.text: Expectation vs reality. The water wasn't as nice. But still a good place to chill in the afternoon with a few drinks and daybed. There's even a few resident dogs who will come by and keep you company for awhile. Several f&b operators running the beach with some water sports like jetski, para sailing, banana boat etc. Easy to get to if youre staying in sunset town. Its about a 25mins grab ride and costs about 100-105k dong. Shower is available just need to order food from the f&b where you have the daybed.

reviews.author: V -
reviews.text: For anyone concerned about trash: Sao Beach really is as beautiful as it looks in my photos. Yes, there are a few areas with litter, mostly near undeveloped or empty properties, but it’s no worse than many other beaches in Southeast Asia. Especially after rainy season. Overall, the beach is stunning, the water is clean (no trash floating in it), and there are plenty of great cafés and restaurants at reasonable prices. I’m not sugarcoating anything, waste management is a real issue across Asia. But Sao Beach is nowhere near as bad as some reviews make it sound. In fact, it’s one of the most beautiful beaches I’ve ever visited. The areas with sunbeds, cafés, and restaurants are well-maintained, clean, and spotless.

reviews.author: Mor Efraim
reviews.text: The beach is absolutely stunning. White sand, amazing water, Beautiful clean nature.. I can spend hours here. One thing to be aware of- they gonna charge you 100k for bed even if you order to drink from the restaurant. It’s about 30 minutes drive from centre.

reviews.author: Pyae Pyae Nyein San
reviews.text: Water is clear but so many dried leaves are there as a trash. Should maintain well with that kind of clear water and white & soft sand

reviews.author: Anna Karasova
reviews.text: The beach is clean and not very deep, with some fish and crabs visible. It’s a bit pricey but not as much as 150,000 dong. Sunbeds and umbrellas are included.

reviews.author: SB
reviews.text: A decent beach but no more. Just like Khem beach down the road it’s good but it’s not paradise.

reviews.author: Time2bookatrip. com
reviews.text: One of our biggest disappointment of the island the water is blue but not that clear and full of garbage and pollution all over the beach. It used to be wild and now there are some beach club and they’re not cleaning the beaches. It’s very sad to see this to enter now you need to pay 60,000 dong but there are some activities like JetSki and other.

reviews.author: pleurat asllani
reviews.text: To summarize the waste situation in a few words: There is a large amount of trash on the beach, though it is mostly concentrated away from the rented sunbeds. It is a paradox where you must intentionally ignore the litter to enjoy the beautiful scenery. I believe that if the locals worked together, this problem could be managed effectively, yet there seems to be a clear lack of collective initiative. The 2 pictures show it best Positive note, street vendors are frequent and offer a variety of fruits and snacks. Visitors should be aware that the public amenities are POOR.

reviews.author: Kathy E. Sanati
reviews.text: This is a gorgeous beach but not very big. In my opinion NOT the best in Phu Quoc and yes, it is full of trash! PQ island has a list of “ best beaches” but for some reason, the best ones I have found are the ones off the map that I accidently found exploring. The unknown beaches. This one was crowded and small. Took me 40 min both ways to walk it entirely. Again,I agree with other reviews that it is full of trash. Most public beaches in Vietnam are. The only clean areas belong to resorts but this beach has mostly beach clubs that only keep their spot clean. Others businesses don’t bother .The swings and decorative bridge to your left belong yo the beach club bar and you have to pay to take pictures there! You also have to pay for bathrooms and showers… what a joke! There is a Highland Coffee shop( popular coffee chain in Vietnam) right at the entrance!

reviews.author: Gabor Horvath
reviews.text: Beautiful, clean, white - perfect! I've never seen such a clean and tidy beach as this one! The water is great for swimming, and on the water there is a charming floating restaurant (Bè Nắng - Quán Ăn Trên Bè) that offers some of the finest seafood in the city in a magical setting! This beach is a must-see! Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Alice OP
reviews.text: I was a bit unsure whether to visit after reading mixed reviews, but I’m really happy I did. This is a genuinely beautiful beach with clear, dreamy water, where you can even see fishes. The weather forecast was 27°C and partially cloudy, yet the sea still looked amazing. The sand is white, very fine, and soft, making it a lovely place to relax. We entered through Sao Beach “Grab” benches (where the taxi driver drops you off) and walked straight to the beach. There was no entrance fee, as this is a free public beach. You can also park your car or scooter at the same spot where taxis drop you off. If you continue walking toward Highland Coffee and stay on the right side of it, facing the sea, especially in the morning, the water is calm and the air feels fresher, as the gasoline smell from jet skis and boats comes from the other side. When water sports become more active, the left side is mainly used for those activities (and they don not care) and isn’t ideal for swimming. Therefore, we recommend going in the morning and staying on the right side. We simply laid our towels down and enjoyed the moment (there are sunbeds for rent available). Aside from stopping at Highland Coffee, we didn’t eat or drink elsewhere, but there are restaurants available; we’d still recommend bringing your own picnic. There are no showers, and visiting early helps avoid the busier water-sport hours. The beach wasn’t crowded during our visit, which made it even more enjoyable. There are a few dogs around, but they are friendly and don’t approach unless you have food. While there is plenty of litter along the coastline and no waste bins, it doesn’t take away from the natural beauty of the beach, though it’s something that sadly the locals are not taking care of. Overall, despite the waste situation, this beach is absolutely worth visiting—at least for a short stop and a swim. With its clear water and soft white sand, it is even nicer than Khem Beach and could easily be the most beautiful beach in Phu Quoc. 🌴

reviews.author: Jonny Carey
reviews.text: Very pleasant afternoon spent here. Not sure what people mean by an entry fee, you need to pay to use sunbeds etc - that's the case worldwide.

reviews.author: Cristina Popa
reviews.text: Nice white sandy beach clear water, bats and testaurants on the beach trees and palm trees on the beach for shadow. nice experience

reviews.author: Nicole Lee
reviews.text: Loved Sao beach! Crystal clear waters and fine sand. Unfortunately, the day I went, it was windy so the waters were a little choppy. I access the beach through Chezdugong Restaurant by accident (my Grab driver dropped me off here instead of at the public beach access), but I was glad he did. There was a $60K VND entrance fee, but it could be applied towards food and drinks. You can rent towels and a lounge chair for $150K. The food wasn’t great, but the drinks were. And they have servers walking up and down the beach to take your order. I decided to stay here instead of the public area bc it felt more peaceful and less busy. Ended up spending the whole day here—it was so nice!

reviews.author: Gianluca Lietaert
reviews.text: Sao Beach is easily one of the most beautiful beaches in Phu Quoc, and it absolutely deserves its reputation for soft white sand and calm, turquoise water. The bay is naturally sheltered, making the sea shallow and peaceful perfect for swimming, relaxing, or just floating around while enjoying the view. On a sunny day, the colors are stunning and feel almost postcard-perfect. There are plenty of beach restaurants and lounges where you can sit back with a drink or enjoy fresh seafood, and prices are generally reasonable for a popular tourist area. The atmosphere during the day is laid-back and enjoyable, and it’s easy to spend several hours here without feeling rushed. It’s also a great spot for families, as the water stays calm and safe. However, the biggest downside and the reason this isn’t a 5-star review is cleanliness. Unfortunately, large parts of the beach are very dirty, with visible plastic waste, bottles, and general litter scattered along the sand and sometimes even in the water. This takes away from what could otherwise be a truly pristine beach experience. For a destination of this popularity and natural beauty, this is disappointing and avoidable. Sao Beach is still very much worth visiting for its scenery and relaxed vibe, but it would benefit enormously from better waste management, regular clean-ups, and more responsibility from both vendors and visitors. With proper care, this could easily be one of the best beaches in Vietnam. A great beach — just not treated with the respect it deserves.

reviews.author: Анна Архипова
reviews.text: This place is a must visit to understand the human impact on the planet. Haven’t seen anything more sad that this marvellous stunning beach fully covered in rubbish that no one cares to clean. Be very careful while walking there.
